highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.116 Released
highlights.summary: This release brings significant performance improvements to `/resume` on large sessions — up to 67% faster for sessions over 40MB. MCP startup is also faster when multiple stdio servers are configured, as resource template listing is now deferred until first use. These changes are especially helpful if you work on long-running projects with many saved sessions.

highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.117 Released
highlights.summary: Version 2.1.117 adds support for forked subagents on external builds via the `CLAUDE_CODE_FORK_SUBAGENT=1` environment variable, and agent frontmatter `mcpServers` are now loaded for main-thread agent sessions when using `--agent`. Model selections made with `/model` now persist across restarts even when a project pins a default, giving you more consistent control over your setup.

highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.113 Released
highlights.summary: A major under-the-hood change: Claude Code's CLI now spawns a native binary for your platform instead of bundled JavaScript, which should improve startup speed and reliability. A new `sandbox.network.deniedDomains` setting lets you block specific domains even if a broader wildcard rule would otherwise allow them, giving you finer-grained network control. This is a great update for teams with strict security requirements.

highlights.title: Introducing Claude Opus 4.7
highlights.summary: Anthropic has officially announced Claude Opus 4.7, a new model that Claude Code can leverage for more complex tasks. If you noticed the 'temporarily unavailable' error for Opus 4.7 in auto mode (fixed in v2.1.112), this is the model behind that fix. Check the announcement to understand its capabilities and how it fits into your Claude Code workflow.

highlights.title: Claude Code to be removed from Anthropic's Pro plan?
highlights.summary: A discussion is circulating about whether Anthropic may remove Claude Code access from its standard Pro subscription tier. This is worth tracking if you rely on Claude Code through a Pro plan, as it could affect your costs and access. No official confirmation yet — keep an eye on Anthropic's announcements for clarity.

highlights.title: Mastering Claude Code in 30 minutes
highlights.summary: This YouTube tutorial promises a concise, focused walkthrough of Claude Code's core features in just 30 minutes — ideal if you want to get productive quickly without sitting through a multi-hour course. It's a great starting point to learn how to navigate the CLI, run commands, and use Claude Code effectively in your day-to-day workflow.

highlights.title: Introducing Claude Code (Official)
highlights.summary: The official Anthropic YouTube video introducing Claude Code is a great reference for understanding the tool's core purpose and design philosophy straight from the source. If you're new to Claude Code or want to explain it to a colleague, this video provides a clear, authoritative overview. Pair it with the latest release notes to see how much the product has grown since launch.

tips_of_the_day: Use the `sandbox.network.deniedDomains` setting (added in v2.1.113) to explicitly block sensitive internal endpoints even when a broad wildcard is needed — this gives you a safety net without sacrificing flexibility.

If your `/resume` command feels slow on large projects, update to Claude Code v2.1.116 immediately; sessions over 40MB are now up to 67% faster to reload, saving you time every time you return to a paused task.

Pin your preferred model once using `/model`, then rely on the new persistence behavior in v2.1.117 so your choice survives restarts — no more re-selecting after every session, even in projects that define a default model.
